finding: Stopped is all-absorbing ((Failed|Stopped,_)=>NoOp) with no exit but ClearFailed. The singleton row defaults to state='stopped' (migration 133), so boot -> ensure_restored -> from_persisted('stopped') -> Stopped, and every StartedOk/Crashed/Ok/Unhealthy is a NoOp. Machine never becomes Healthy, is_failed() always false, flap/give-up never fires: a crash-looping whisper-server respawns on every transcribe with no cap/backoff. Same wedge after admin_stop/idle-reap. Unit tests use new() (Starting), missing the from_persisted('stopped') path.

MicButton has no unmount cleanup (no useEffect returning cancelRecording). Recording resources live in module scope, so navigating away mid-recording leaves the MediaStream live (mic indicator ON) and elapsedTimer firing until max_clip_seconds (up to 3600s), which then auto-transcribes into a conversation the user already left. Privacy defect.

extract_tar_gz unpacks the binary into the FINAL cache_dir; if a later entry fails the fn returns Err but the binary is already on disk and download_with_progress does not remove cache_dir on extract failure. A retry short-circuits on binary_path.exists() and returns the incomplete (lib-less) install as cached -> version row registered -> spawn fails. Re-download can't self-heal.

start_or_join terminal-replacement is check-then-insert across an await: two concurrent re-POSTs for a terminal key both observe terminal, both spawn_runner + insert -> TWO live runners writing the same shared temp archive path concurrently (corrupt archive/sha mismatch) and racing the UNIQUE(version,platform,arch,backend) constraint. The overwritten runner keeps running invisibly.

monitor_health probes only GET / -> Ok|Unhealthy; a runtime process death is classified Unhealthy, never Crashed. The SlidingWindow flap counter only advances on HealthEvent::Crashed (emitted solely from do_start failure). So a runtime crash-loop of an instance that once started healthy never trips give-up. Independent of the Stopped-wedge.

Transcribe route caps body at VOICE_TRANSCRIBE_BODY_LIMIT=64MiB, but voice_runtime_settings.max_upload_bytes allows up to 256MiB and the handler compares against that setting. Raising max_upload_bytes above 64MiB yields a 413 from the body-limit layer before the handler's VOICE_CLIP_TOO_LARGE runs. Effective cap is silently min(64MiB, setting); the settings range is misleading/unreachable.

update_settings validates model + numeric caps but NOT language; stored verbatim (VARCHAR(20) only). An invalid language is accepted 200, then may be rejected by validate_argv_value at auto-start, surfacing as an opaque 503 VOICE_RUNTIME_UNAVAILABLE on next transcribe instead of a clean 400 at configure time. Not injection (argv tokens), a validation gap.

ensure_model_with_progress treats an all-zero sha256 pin (and a model with no KNOWN_MODEL_SHA256 entry) as verification-skip (warn + accept). Dormant today (all 4 models have real pins + a test), but a future unpinned/placeholder model would install network bytes with no integrity check and load them into the whisper-server subprocess. Prefer fail-closed.

`while let Ok(Some(field)) = multipart.next_field().await` discards the Err arm: a mid-stream multipart read error falls through to VOICE_NO_AUDIO ('missing file field'), masking a truncated/aborted upload as a missing-field 400.

The 'requesting' state (awaiting getUserMedia) is unescapable: MicButton shows only a spinner with no cancel while isBusy, no timeout on the getUserMedia promise, and cancelRecording early-returns unless status==='recording'. If the user ignores the permission prompt (promise stays pending) the button is stuck spinning with no abort.

loadActive() does NOT self-gate on Permissions.VoiceAdminRead unlike every sibling voice store. Registered globally in module.tsx stores[], its init() runs for ALL users and calls ApiClient.Voice.listVersionDownloads() (requires VoiceAdminRead) -> every non-admin (Users hold only voice::transcribe) triggers a 403 on app load, violating the no-403 rule.

No focus management: on Stop/Cancel the button unmounts (replaced by a non-focusable transcribing div), then the idle mic returns; focus falls to <body>, and the text appended to the composer is never announced.

The recording aria-live='polite' region contains the per-second elapsed timer ('Recording, m:ss'), so screen readers re-announce it every second (flood). A live region should announce discrete state changes, not a ticking counter.

On status==='error' the component falls through to the idle branch and renders NO aria-live region; errorMessage (commented 'for the aria-live region') is never surfaced to AT. A screen-reader user gets no announcement that dictation failed (only the visual toast).

The not-ready posture wraps a disabled Button in a Tooltip; a disabled button is out of tab order, so the remediation guidance ('contact an administrator') is mouse-hover-only. Keyboard/SR users get only aria-label 'unavailable' with no next step.

aria-live regions are conditionally mounted per status-branch rather than persistent nodes that update; a live region inserted already containing its text is often NOT announced (SR needs a mutation to an existing region). On-mount announcements are unreliable.

Instance lifecycle mutations emit no sync event and there is no VoiceInstance sync entity. restart/stop/do_start/reaper idle-unload all UPDATE voice_runtime_instance with no sync_publish; the frontend VoiceInstance store only listens on sync:reconnect. An open /settings/voice instance card goes stale until reload/SSE reconnect. (See DRIFT-1.2 — instance sync was intentionally omitted; audit flags the staleness.)

Model download streams chunks but writes via blocking std::fs::File::write_all inside the async loop (plus create/flush). For 'small' (~466MB) that is hundreds of blocking write syscalls on a tokio worker; invoked from do_start it also holds START_LOCK, blocking an executor thread for the whole download. Use tokio::fs / spawn_blocking.

audio = Some(bytes.to_vec()) copies the already-buffered multipart Bytes into a fresh Vec then forwards by value — one avoidable full copy of the whole clip (up to 64MB). reqwest multipart Part::bytes accepts Bytes; pass it directly.

Transcription failure surfaces the raw backend error: `Transcription failed: ${err.message}`, leaking internal jargon/infra ('whisper inference request failed: ... http://127.0.0.1:PORT/inference: connection refused', 'HTTP 500') to end users. Show a clean user message; log the detail.
resampleLinear is verified only by output length; the upsample test feeds all-zeros so interpolation correctness is unverified. A resampler returning zeros of the right length would pass.

stub-whisper-server was added as a workspace member but is referenced by NO test/dev-dependency; the real spawn->health->/inference transcription path it exists to exercise is untested. Wire it into a MockReleaseServer + tests/voice/ (phase 8).

No integration/handler test (no tests/voice/). Caps enforcement (VOICE_CLIP_TOO_LARGE/TOO_LONG), multipart parsing, disabled-guard 409, and forward_to_whisper are untested critical paths (only pure helpers are unit-tested).

The security-critical download path (sha256 verify vs KNOWN_MODEL_SHA256, delete-on-mismatch, MAX_MODEL_BYTES cap, placeholder-skip) has no test. A regression that skips/inverts the hash check would ship green.
downmixToMono (channel averaging + mono passthrough) feeds every transcription but has no test.
Casing inconsistency: 'Delete Runtime Version' / 'Set as Default' (Title Case) vs sibling sentence-case titles/buttons ('Installed runtimes', 'Check for updates').
Tooltip 'Make this the runtime new sessions use' is grammatically awkward (missing relative pronoun). Reword e.g. 'Make this the default runtime for new sessions'.

subscribeToKey idempotency guard (if sseAborts.has(key) return) can be bypassed by a rapid double-call because the map entry is set later in the deferred __init callback, not synchronously -> two concurrent SSE subscriptions for one key. Set a synchronous placeholder before the async subscribe.

AUDIT NOTE (perms-authz, clean): every voice REST endpoint carries the correct RequirePermissions gate (transcribe/capability=voice::transcribe; admin/version/model/instance=voice::admin::read|manage); migration 134 grants only voice::transcribe to Users; base_url only exposed on admin endpoints. No authz bypass found beyond the frontend 403-storm above.
